412.title: Crimes That Shook Australia
412.description: Journalist Stan Grant presents this programme that takes a look at some high-profile crimes. Each episode focuses on a particular crime as seen through the eyes of the people who were at the heart of the tragedies. Interviews, dramatic re-enactments and archival footage are used to detail the events leading up to each crime, the crime itself and the aftermath of the unlawful act. It's all used to help piece together the murders, which include the 1996 Port Arthur massacre that resulted in dozens of deaths and serious injuries.

7119.title: Crimes That Shook Australia
7119.description: The story of John Bunting and a trio of his associates who splattered the south Australian district of Adelaide blood-red with 12 horrific slayings in the 1990s.

7157.title: Crimes That Shook Australia
7157.description: In December 1987, Frank Vitkovic kills nine people in Melbourne's Australia Post headquarters. The day becomes a milestone along the road to stricter gun controls in Australia.

7158.title: Crimes That Shook Australia
7158.description: In the Belanglo State Forest, in November 2010, Matthew Milat kills a school friend in cold blood. In doing so, he satisfies his desire to follow in the footsteps of his notorious uncle, Ivan.
